commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(telegram): drop unsupported local markdown link hrefs in rich HTML (fixes #94117)
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes #94117. - Telegram `sendRichMessage` rejects rich payloads when a Markdown link href is a local or relative path, returning `RICH_MESSAGE_URL_INVALID` and dropping the entire final reply. - Skip emitting `` only for hrefs outside Telegram's supported inline-link set. Supported schemes — `http://`, `https://`, `tg://`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, and in-document `#anchor` — stay clickable; everything else (local/relative paths) degrades to the visible link label (or existing file-ref `` wrapping when applicable). This matches the full Telegram rich-link contract rather than a narrower subset: `mailto:`, `tel:`, and `#...` anchors are preserved (main's own `format.test.ts` already keeps a rich `` anchor), so the fix suppresses only the hrefs Telegram actually rejects. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: Telegram final replies containing Markdown links to local filesystem paths fail delivery with `RICH_MESSAGE_URL_INVALID` instead of degrading the unsupported href to visible text, while supported link targets stay clickable. Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux (WSL2), `extensions/telegram` formatter path only (no live Telegram bot credentials required for this sanitizer contract). Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts node -e " const { markdownToTelegramRichHtml } = await import('./extensions/telegram/src/format.ts'); const cases = { local: '[scripts/yougile.py](/home/user/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/yougile.py#L41)', relative: '[config](./openclaw.json)', https: '[docs](https://example.com/docs)', tg: '[user](tg://user?id=123)', mailto: '[support](mailto:user@example.com)', tel: '[call](tel:+123456789)', anchor: '[back](#top)', }; for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(cases)) console.log(k+':', markdownToTelegramRichHtml(v)); " ``` Evidence after fix: All 49 cases in `extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts` pass, including `keeps unsupported markdown link hrefs as visible text in rich HTML`. Local/relative href output contains no `scripts/yougile.py relative: config https: docs tg: user mailto: support tel: call anchor: back ``` What was not tested: Live Telegram `sendRichMessage` roundtrip against the Telegram API (formatter contract only). ## Tests and validation - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts` — 49 passed. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? Yes Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No Highest-risk area: Telegram rich-message link rendering for non-http hrefs Mitigation: Only suppresses anchors for hrefs Telegram already rejects; labels remain visible and supported `http`/`https`/`tg`/`mailto`/`tel`/`#anchor` links are unchanged. ## Current review state Next action: Re-review — broadened the supported-link allow-list (`mailto`/`tel`/`#anchor`) so the fix is no longer narrower than the Telegram rich-link contract previously flagged by ClawSweeper. Waiting on: Maintainer review Bot comments addressed: ClawSweeper "narrower than Telegram's rich-link contract / superseded by #94118" — addressed by matching the full supported-scheme set. AI-assisted: yes
target.author: pick-cat
